name: Security Infringement
description: Infringement scoring overrides for Security patents. Covers network security, authentication, cryptography, and secure computing.
scoringGuidance: Security patents often describe generic mechanisms (authentication, encryption, access control) that map to widely-deployed product features.
Products claiming compliance with security standards (FIPS, Common Criteria, SOC2) necessarily implement specific cryptographic and access control mechanisms.
Firewall and IDS/IPS patents use abstract terms like 'packet inspection' and 'policy enforcement' that map to concrete product features in every network security product.
Authentication patents may apply broadly — any product with login, SSO, or MFA inherently implements authentication flows described in many patents.
necessaryImplicationGuidance: Any enterprise security product necessarily implements: (1) authentication/authorization, (2) encrypted communications (TLS/IPsec), (3) logging/audit trails, (4) policy management. Any firewall/NGFW necessarily implements: (1) stateful packet inspection, (2) rule matching engines, (3) session tracking, (4) NAT. Any VPN product necessarily implements: (1) tunnel establishment, (2) key exchange, (3) encapsulation/decapsulation.
